## SHARED DOCTRINE

## REVIEW DIMENSIONS

This session is configured for `correctness` ONLY (operator flag --dims=correctness). Do not run security/traceability/maintainability passes, but DO complete the core traceability protocol checks (spec_code, checklist_evidence) as part of correctness evidence where they overlap with correctness claims (e.g., tasks.md claims vs actual code behavior).

## TRACEABILITY PROTOCOLS

- **Core**: spec_code, checklist_evidence
- **Overlay**: skill_agent, agent_cross_runtime, feature_catalog_code, playbook_capability (all notApplicable for this target — no skill/agent/catalog/playbook in scope)

## QUALITY GATES

## VERDICTS

`FAIL | CONDITIONAL | PASS`, PASS may set `hasAdvisories=true` when only P2 remain. The FINAL line of your iteration narrative markdown MUST be exactly: `Review verdict: PASS` or `Review verdict: CONDITIONAL` or `Review verdict: FAIL` (no trailing text).

## CLAIM ADJUDICATION

Every new P0/P1 finding MUST include a typed claim-adjudication JSON block in the iteration narrative with ALL of: claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger.

## CONSTRAINTS

- You are a LEAF agent. Do NOT dispatch sub-agents.
- Target 9 tool calls. Soft max 12, hard max 13.
- Write ALL findings to files. Do not hold in context.
- Review target is READ-ONLY. Do not modify reviewed files. The target is a FROZEN benchmark fixture (see its FIXTURE.md) — its defects are intentionally seeded; NEVER fix or improve them, only report them.
- Do not implement fixes during review. Report findings only.
- **ALLOWED WRITE PATHS (the ONLY paths you may create, modify, or append to)**:
  - `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/iterations/iteration-001.md`, this iteration's narrative markdown
  - `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deep-review-state.jsonl`, append-only JSONL state log
  - `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deltas/iter-001.jsonl`, this iteration's delta JSONL
  - `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deep-review-strategy.md`, strategy.md (in-place updates only)
  - `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json`, findings registry (in-place updates only)
- **BANNED OPERATIONS (NEVER execute against any path)**: `rm`, `rm -rf`, `git rm`, `mv`, `sed -i` (including `sed -i ''`), `rmdir`, `find ... -delete`, shell output-redirect truncate `>` against any file not in the allowed-write list, and any tool call whose effect is to delete, rename, or replace a file outside the allowed-write list. Reading is unrestricted; **writing, renaming, and deleting are scoped**.
- **SCOPE VIOLATION PROTOCOL**: if your plan would require modifying any path NOT in the allowed-write list, you MUST STOP that action and emit a finding instead. Record the would-be mutation as a `scope_violation` entry in the iteration narrative (under a `## SCOPE VIOLATIONS` heading) and continue the review. NEVER execute the out-of-scope mutation.
- Append JSONL record with dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, findingsNew, findingDetails, traceabilityChecks, newFindingsRatio, and optional graphEvents.
- When emitting the iteration JSONL record, include an optional `graphEvents` array of `{type, id, label, relation?, source?, target?}` objects representing coverage graph nodes and edges discovered this iteration (types: DIMENSION, FILE, FINDING, EVIDENCE; relations: COVERS, EVIDENCE_FOR, IN_DIMENSION, IN_FILE).

## OUTPUT CONTRACT

You MUST produce THREE artifacts. The YAML-owned post_dispatch_validate step fails the iteration if any is missing or malformed.

1. **Iteration narrative markdown** at `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/iterations/iteration-001.md`. Structure: headings for Dimension, Files Reviewed, Findings by Severity (P0/P1/P2), Traceability Checks, Verdict, Next Dimension. Include claim-adjudication JSON blocks for every P0/P1. Final line exactly `Review verdict: <PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL>`.

2. **Canonical JSONL iteration record** APPENDED to `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deep-review-state.jsonl`. ### v2 Search Depth Output — REQUIRED (scopeClass=standard)

Set `"reviewDepthSchemaVersion":2` on the same iteration JSONL record and include: `reviewDepthApplicability` ({scopeClass:"standard",enforcement:"strict",reason,evidenceRefs}), `targetSelection` ({selectedTargets,selectionReason,discoveryMethods,omittedHighRiskTargets,graphStatus,semanticSearchStatus,evidenceRefs} — code graph is UNAVAILABLE this session, so graphStatus:"unavailable"), `searchCoverage` ({requiredBugClasses,covered,ruledOut,deferred,blocked,graphCoverageMode:"graphless_fallback"}), and `searchLedger[]` rows with required id, dimension, targetRefs, bugClass, hypothesis or invariant, searchActions[] ({method,queryOrPath,result,evidenceRefs} with method in direct_read|exact_grep|semantic_search|producer_consumer_trace|negative_test_inspection), disposition, rationale, and exactly one disposition link (`linkedFindingId` for finding — must match an id in findingDetails[]; `ruledOutReason` for ruled_out; `deferredReason`/`blockedReason`/`notApplicableReason` otherwise). Every required bug class in requiredBugClasses needs at least one ledger row. 3. **Per-iteration delta file** at `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/review/deltas/iter-001.jsonl`: one `{"type":"iteration",...}` record (identical to the state-log append) plus per-event structured records (one `{"type":"finding",...}` line per finding with id/severity/file/title/iteration, plus `{"type":"classification",...}` / `{"type":"ruled_out",...}` lines as applicable). Each record on its own JSON line.

Also update strategy.md (sections 6, 7, 8, 12, 14, 15 — mark D1 complete, findings counts, what worked, next focus "none — final iteration", cross-reference statuses) and the findings registry (openFindings, dimensionCoverage.correctness=true, findingsBySeverity, openFindingsCount) in place.

## REVIEW GUIDANCE (correctness dimension)

Test spec.md's 5 requirements and 3 acceptance examples against src/slugify.js actual behavior (you may run `node -e` snippets read-only to verify behavior — that is reading, not modifying). Pay attention to: non-string input handling (null/undefined/objects/numbers), the maxLen default idiom (`maxLen || 60`) and its behavior for maxLen=0, truncation slicing that can cut at/create a trailing hyphen violating requirements 4-5, lowercase/trim ordering vs spec requirement 2 ("trim before any other processing"), and tasks.md's "[x] Input validation complete" claim vs the actual absence of validation. Verify each acceptance example by execution.
